stem: A decision-maker must choose among 3 drug treatment options. The probability of favorable patient genotype is uncertain, estimated between 41% and 55%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Drug A: $204K in good patient genotype, $25K in bad patient genotype. Drug B: $141K in good patient genotype, $55K in bad patient genotype. Drug C: $191K in good patient genotype, $37K in bad patient genotype. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.48: best option is Drug A with EV=$110.9K. The optimal choice switches: Drug C at P(good)=0.41, Drug A at P(good)=0.55. EV range of best option: [100.1, 123.5].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
The optimal choice switches: Drug C at P(good)=0.41, Drug A at P(good)=0.55.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
